ddls:
CREATE TABLE "control_elements" (
  "obj_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each control element */,
  "owner_obj_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the owner object of the control element */,
  "owner_prop_id" INTEGER /* Property ID of the owner to which this control element belongs */,
  "coll_ord" INTEGER /* Ordering of the control element in the graphical interface */,
  "cls_id" INTEGER /* Class ID indicating the type of control element */,
  "graphical_name" TEXT /* Graphical name used for display in control systems */,
  "signal_label" TEXT /* Signal label associated with the control element */,
  "connected" INTEGER /* Indicates if the control element is currently connected */,
  "test_point" INTEGER /* Indicates if the control element has a test point configured */,
  "logged" INTEGER /* Indicates if the control element is set to be logged */,
  "graphical_source" INTEGER /* Source identifier for the graphical representation */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the control element */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the control element was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "maintenance_due_date" TEXT /* Date when the next maintenance is due */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the control element */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the control element */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the control element */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "calibration_due_date" TEXT /* Date when the next calibration is due */,
  "alarms" INTEGER /* Number of active alarms */,
  "last_alarm_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the last alarm */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the control element */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("obj_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_control_elements_owner_obj_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_obj_id") REFERENCES owners ("owner_obj_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_control_elements_cls_id FOREIGN KEY ("cls_id") REFERENCES classifications ("cls_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "owners" (
  "owner_obj_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each owner object */,
  "owner_name" TEXT /* Name of the owner object */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the owner object */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the owner object */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person for the owner object */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the contact person */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact person */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the owner object belongs */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the owner object was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the owner object was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("owner_obj_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "classifications" (
  "cls_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each classification */,
  "cls_name" TEXT /* Name of the classification */,
  "cls_description" TEXT /* Description of the classification */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the classification was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the classification was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the classification is active */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or remarks about the classification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cls_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_records" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "obj_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the control element being logged */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Description of the logged event or status */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who logged the event */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log (e.g., 'info', 'warning', 'error') */,
  "severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the log (e.g., 1 for low, 5 for high) */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the logged issue has been resolved */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the issue was resolved */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on how the issue was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_records_obj_id FOREIGN KEY ("obj_id") REFERENCES control_elements ("obj_id")
)
